page: Amazon Web Services r8g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton4 processors utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores based on the Neoverse-V2 design, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 7394.89 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 463.55 GB/sec for large-block reads. Multi-core compute capabilities are strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25270.0 and a Passmark CPU physics score of 15853.19. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 132.23 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier. However, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1879.0, and LLM text generation on small models is relatively weak at 53.81 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based architecture offers high memory density and memory bandwidth per unit of resource, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. Tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data, and the lack of x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. Given its massive memory capacity and top-tier database and memory bandwidth benchmarks, this instance is optimized for in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, software compilation, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.16xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth, with cached reads reaching 7394.89 GB/sec, and exceptional database performance, including 132.23 million Redis SET operations per second. While multi-core performance is strong, single-core benchmarks and small-model LLM text generation are moderate to weak. Lacking AVX/AMX instruction sets and local storage, this instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.16xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 Graviton physical cores, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.16xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and dedicated core density for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching 7394.89 GB/sec for cached reads
Delivers 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and x86-specific vector instruction sets
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
